headline: Iran ceasefire collapses over weekend; Brent tops $90, Iraq exports hit too
gist: WTI $83.57 (+2.19%), Brent >$90 (session high $90.79); US-Iran conflict widened over the weekend to Jordan, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman; drone strike shut Iraq's Basra export terminal; Strait of Hormuz transit collapsed to 4-6 ships/day from ~20; war-risk insurance 3-10% of vessel value

headline: Reliance Q1 FY27 beats: record EBITDA Rs54,067cr, PAT ~Rs23,196cr
gist: Revenue +24.5-25% YoY, record EBITDA Rs54,067cr, net profit ~Rs23,196cr, beat Street estimates; O2C +30% YoY, Jio revenue +11.8% YoY (533mn subs), Retail +12%; brokerages (JPMorgan, Jefferies, Citi, CLSA) raise FY27-29 estimates 6-8%

headline: ICICI Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank beat Q1; HDFC Bank light miss on NII
gist: ICICI Bank PAT +15.9% YoY to Rs14,804.5cr (beat), NPAs improved; Kotak Mahindra PAT +25.6% YoY to Rs4,122.96cr (beat); Axis Bank PAT +22.5% YoY to Rs7,114cr but NIM at cycle low 3.46%; HDFC Bank PAT +5% YoY to Rs19,059.7cr, NII +6.7% (below estimates), slippages rose

headline: Wall St chip selloff drags Asia lower; Nikkei -4%, Kospi -4.5% Monday
gist: Fri Jul 17 US close: S&P -1.0% to 7,475.69, Nasdaq -1.4% to 25,520.24, Dow -0.8% on AI/chip-stock selloff (hyperscaler capex concerns); Nikkei -4.03% Fri (chip names Kioxia -16%, Tokyo Electron -8%), Kospi -4.5% Monday into bear-market territory; GIFT Nifty near-flat/slightly weak

headline: Gold, silver firm as Mideast conflict widens; Fed-hawkish USD caps upside
gist: GC=F $4,031.4 (+0.31%), broke through $4,006-4,013 supply zone, testing range highs; SI=F $57.46 (+2.01%); early Asian dip on Fed Sept rate-hike odds (~59%) reversed as Iran conflict widened to Iraq/Jordan/Kuwait, reviving safe-haven bid; DXY firm near 100.8-100.9

headline: Nifty 50, Bank Nifty open lower on risk-off despite domestic earnings beats
gist: Nifty 24,194.5 (-0.57%), Bank Nifty 57,795.7 (-1.24%), Sensex ~77,702.9 (-0.57%, tracking Nifty); risk-off open driven by oil spike + global chip-selloff carryover outweighing strong Reliance/ICICI/Kotak Q1 beats; India VIX +2.1% to 13.15; FII net sold Rs376.4cr, DII net bought Rs1,017.9cr on Jul 17

headline: UPDATE: Iran conflict enters 9th strike night; Brent tops $90, 3rd US serviceman killed
gist: 9th consecutive night of US strikes on Iran per CENTCOM; 3rd US service member reported killed; WTI $83.74 (+2.40%) broke above 83.69-83.75 supply to a fresh day high ($83.80), Brent still >$90; oil-driven inflation fear now offsetting the usual safe-haven bid in gold

headline: UPDATE: Private bank selloff deepens on Q1 NIM compression
gist: Bank Nifty -1.42% to 57,692.9 (widened from -1.24% at open); HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra down as much as 5% intraday extending the Q1 NIM-compression reaction; index held above 57,577-57,623 demand zone, rejected twice near 57,770-57,796

headline: UPDATE: Gold fades from breakout, defends $4,000
gist: GC=F $4,013.30 (-0.14%), pulled back below VWAP after testing the $4,031 range high; oil-driven inflation fear (yields up) now offsetting the Iran-conflict safe-haven bid, per FXStreet's 'tug-of-war' framing; SI=F $56.79 (+0.82%), also pulling back off session high $57.33
